prompt: I'm training a ~1B parameter E-Branchformer encoder-decoder ASR model in ESPnet on about 40k hours of speech, batch around 256, AdamW peak lr 2e-4, warmup 5000 steps - same warmup I use for my text LLM runs. Loss goes NaN during warmup almost every time. My text recipes never do this. What's different about speech and what should I set?
assertions: Identifies warmup as too short for speech and recommends 25K-60K steps or staged/piecewise warmup
References OWSM v3.1 (60K piecewise warmup at 1B scale) or another published speech recipe by name
Mentions speech Adam conventions (beta2 0.98 / eps 1e-6 from Whisper) or CTC-related stabilization
Explains why speech differs from text (continuous noisy features / encoder-decoder dynamics), not just generic advice
Keeps peak LR ~2e-4 as plausible for 1B speech rather than reflexively slashing it to text-SFT levels
